tips: [BOB] Run /setup-rules after installation to generate project rules and AGENTS.md
[BOB] Use /spec for features AND bug fixes — structured planning, TDD, and code review for both
[BOB] Quick Mode: Just chat for small changes and exploration (all quality hooks still apply)
[BOB] Press Ctrl+R to search command history | Ctrl+C to cancel current operation
[BOB] Press Escape twice to cancel a running operation
[BOB] Check /help for all available commands and shortcuts
[BOB] Use /clear to start fresh while keeping project context loaded
[BOB] Run /ide to enable real-time diagnostics from your IDE
[BOB] Use @file.py to reference files directly in your prompts
[BOB] Bob Memory: Claude remembers your past sessions and relevant context automatically
[BOB] Auto-compaction preserves state seamlessly — no context is ever lost between compaction cycles
[BOB] Context Monitor tracks usage — auto-compaction fires automatically, no context is ever lost
[BOB] Bob Console: Browse your memory, sessions, and specs at localhost:41777
[BOB] Probe: Semantic search that finds code by meaning, not just text matching
[BOB] Context7: Claude fetches up-to-date library docs when you use unfamiliar packages
[BOB] grep-mcp: Search 1M+ public GitHub repos for real-world code patterns and examples
[BOB] TDD Enforcer: Warns when modifying code without failing tests first
[BOB] Quality hooks auto-run on every edit: Python, TypeScript, Go, and more
[BOB] 17 hooks fire across 7 lifecycle events — quality enforcement is automatic and comprehensive
[BOB] Coding standards activate conditionally by file type — Python rules only load for .py files
[BOB] LSP: Language servers provide real-time diagnostics, go-to-definition, and hover info
[BOB] LSP servers (Python, TypeScript, Go) are installed automatically with Bob
[BOB] Add custom rules in .claude/rules/ to guide Claude's behavior
[BOB] MCP servers extend capabilities: web search, memory, docs, IDE integration
[BOB] Add custom MCP servers to .mcp.json, then run /setup-rules to generate documentation
[BOB] playwright-cli enables headless browser automation, testing, and tracing
[BOB] Quality hooks validate your code changes automatically before completion
[BOB] Spec-Driven: /spec → Plan → Approve → Implement → Verify (with feedback loop)
[BOB] Plans live in docs/plans/ as markdown — you can edit them before approval
[BOB] Smart Model Routing: Opus for planning and verification, Sonnet for fast implementation
[BOB] Plan-verifier catches gaps, missing edge cases, and requirement mismatches before coding
[BOB] Verifier sub-agents perform independent code review against the plan before completion
[BOB] Spec stop guard blocks the session from ending until verification is complete
[BOB] Worktree Isolation: /spec can run in isolated git worktrees — main branch stays clean
[BOB] Per-task commits in worktree mode enable git bisect to isolate regressions
[BOB] Rules: Always loaded | Commands: On-demand via /cmd | Skills: Auto-loaded when relevant
[BOB] Add your own rules, commands, and skills — they survive Bob updates
[BOB] Standard rules in ~/.claude/rules/ are updated on install — don't edit them
[BOB] Custom rules in .claude/rules/ are yours — Bob never touches them
[BOB] Skills are auto-loaded when relevant — no need to manually invoke them
[BOB] Skills provide coding standards for Python, TypeScript, Go, testing, and more
[BOB] Create your own skills in .claude/skills/ (avoid standards-* prefix)
[BOB] Run /setup-rules after adding MCP servers or changing project structure
[BOB] /setup-rules discovers undocumented patterns in your codebase and generates rules automatically
[BOB] Share Dashboard → Manage and sync skills across machines and teams in the Console at localhost:41777/#/share
[BOB] /create-skill builds a reusable skill from any topic — explores codebase and creates it interactively
[BOB] Both /spec and Quick Mode benefit from auto-compaction and persistent memory
[BOB] Multi-Session: Run multiple bob sessions in the same project — each is fully isolated
[BOB] Verification loops back automatically if tests fail or issues are found
[BOB] Work is verified before marked complete — no more 'hope it works'
[BOB] /spec explores your codebase and asks clarifying questions before writing the plan
[BOB] Code is auto-formatted, linted, and type-checked on every edit
[BOB] Bob auto-updates to the latest version when launched via the bob command
[BOB] Shell integration: bob command work in bash, fish, and zsh automatically
[BOB] Zero API keys needed — web search and scraping work out of the box
[BOB] All Bob CLI commands support --json for structured, machine-readable output
[BOB] Privacy: No code, files, or project data ever leaves your machine through Bob
[BOB] Bob works with any existing project — no scaffolding, no restructuring required
[BOB] Install a specific version: export VERSION=x.y.z before running the install script
[BOB] Platforms: macOS, Linux, and Windows (WSL2) are all supported
[BOB] Works on macOS, Linux, and Windows (WSL2) — one installer, all platforms
[BOB] codebase-memory-mcp: Code knowledge graph for call tracing, dead code detection, and impact analysis
[BOB] RTK: Token-optimized CLI proxy — saves 60-90% tokens on dev operations, transparent via hooks
[BOB] Spec Workflow Toggles: Configure worktree, questions, approval, and reviewers in Console Settings
[BOB] Skillshare: Share skills, rules, commands, and agents across machines and teams via git
[BOB] Please star the repository: github.com/healthbright/bob
[BOB] Fun fact: Bob is built with Bob — a self-improving development loop

companyAnnouncements: ✈️ Console: http://localhost:41777 | 📋 /spec — features & bugfixes | 📐 /setup-rules — generate rules | 🎯 /create-skill — create skills
